---
title: "HVDC Converter Stations"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

HVDC converter stations convert offshore AC power to HVDC for export and convert HVDC back to AC onshore. They are used in the electricity export architecture.

## Cost Model

The current assumption is based on TenneT programme data for a 2 GW station package.

| Item | Assumption |
|---|---:|
| Standard station capacity | 2 GW |
| Total offshore station package | 3.5 billion EUR |
| Substation portion | 2.0 billion EUR |
| Platform and installation portion | 1.5 billion EUR |
| Linearised cost basis | 1,750 EUR/kW |

All costs are currently assumed to scale linearly with capacity. This is a simplifying assumption and should be revisited if discrete 2 GW block behaviour is explicitly modelled.

## Energy Balance

The offshore conversion chain is included in the offshore substation efficiency assumption. The onshore station is included separately.

| Component | Efficiency |
|---|---:|
| Offshore substation / conversion | 98.5% |
| Onshore substation / conversion | To be confirmed |

## Availability

| Component | Availability |
|---|---:|
| Offshore substation | 98.0% |
| Onshore substation | 98.0% |

## Installation and Mass

The platform and installation portion is currently included in the station package. See [HVDC and Substation Platforms](../platforms/hvdc_and_substation_platforms.qmd) and [Platform and Substation Installation](../offshore_installation/platform_and_substation_installation.qmd).
